query: Clone yourself. Let AI do the work before you ask complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: How to Make an AI Clone of Yourself (Custom GPT Advisors)
results.content:
I highly recommend using your voice for this. Give your hands a break and hit the dictate button. You can also use a speech-to-text software like Wispr Flow, which lets you revise as you talk, adds a bit of formatting (makes bullet lists) and works in any tool you use, not just in ChatGPT.

Tell it what you’re making together. Tell it the purpose, goals, topics, tone and core principals of your AI clone. Have a 10 minute conversation.

It will ask you what to name it. Name it after yourself or your brand (Drew Davis calls his “Drewdini”). It will offer to make an icon. It’s always weird. Better to use your face or your company logo. It’s your AI avatar.

Make it a two-way conversation. Don’t just tell it what to do. Ask it questions. Here are a few good ones: [...] Fix: In “Create” mode, tell it the problem and ask for suggestions. Once you agree on a fix, tell it to update the instructions.

Make those revisions and test again. It’s possible to make a clone quickly, but more effort will give you a smarter clone. You don’t train a team member in half an hour and then expect them to do a great job. The same goes with this synthetic version of you.

ProTip: Train Your Clone on a Schedule. Set a calendar event to test and train your clone for 30 minutes per week, at least for the first month or so.

# How to Make an AI Clone of Yourself (or a GPT Advisor for Anything)

Artificial intelligence (AI)

By Andy Crestodina

If you’ve published content, you have an opportunity.

With nothing more than a ChatGPT Plus account and the content you’ve already produced, you can clone yourself. You can create a custom AI that knows what you know, and gives the advice that you give.

results.title: Can AI Really Handle Customer Complaints? 5 Shocking Insights You Need to Know
results.content:
Contents

##### Sign up to our newsletter

If you’ve ever worked in customer service, you know complaints are inevitable. What separates great businesses from struggling ones isn’t whether they get complaints—it’s how they handle them. Get it right, and you build trust, loyalty, and long-term customers. Get it wrong, and you risk negative reviews, social media backlash, and lost revenue.

Artificial intelligence (AI) is revolutionizing customer interactions. But does it have what it takes to truly resolve complaints? Can AI understand frustration, offer genuine solutions, and keep customers happy?

The short answer? Yes and no. [...] Natural Language Processing (NLP): NLP lets AI understand human language naturally, turning vague complaints into clear, actionable data.
 Sentiment Analysis: This helps AI gauge the emotional context behind complaints, quickly identifying when customers feel frustrated or upset.
 Automated Routing: AI automatically categorizes calls based on keywords or context, routing them to the best-suited agents, resulting in faster resolutions.

For example, if a customer calls and says, “I’ve been overcharged again,” NLP and sentiment analysis instantly categorize it as a billing complaint with high frustration. The system then routes it to an agent specially trained in handling delicate billing issues.

## AI-Powered Chatbots vs. Live Agents: Which Is Better? [...] 🚧 It Struggles with Unique Complaints: AI relies on past data. When something entirely new happens, it can misinterpret the issue. For example, explaining a unique billing error often requires human reasoning and judgment.

🚧 Emotional Situations Need Human Empathy: A chatbot can’t say, “I truly understand your frustration.” Humans connect emotionally in ways AI simply cannot.

🚧 Customers Get Frustrated When Trapped in AI Loops: 60% of consumers still prefer speaking with a human agent, especially for complicated issues.

🚧 Cultural and Language Nuances: Slang, idioms, and cultural references easily confuse AI systems, causing misunderstandings.

> 60% of consumers prefer speaking with a human agent, especially for complicated or sensitive issues. – pwc.com

results.title: Harnessing the power of ai automation for streamlined business operations | NiCE
results.content:
The impact of intelligent automation on customer experience doesn’t end there. AI tools also manage service tickets in customer support systems. They optimize response times by prioritizing issues based on their urgency and relevance, enhancing customer satisfaction. So, whether it’s a simple query or a critical issue, you can trust AI automation to handle it effectively.

## Implementing AI Automation in Your Business [...] AI automation is also enhancing customer experiences. Artificial intelligence models leverage natural language processing to comprehend customer inquiries and preferences, enabling the provision of tailored support and services. It’s like having a personal concierge who knows your tastes and preferences, providing a truly personalized service.

The advancements in conversational AI and virtual assistants are further revolutionizing customer service. These AI-driven chatbots and virtual assistants provide:

It’s like having a 24/7 customer service representative who never gets tired or takes a break! [...] AI technologies in automation utilize learning and decision-making capabilities, allowing them to work independently of human intervention. This independence is achieved through AI techniques like:

These technologies form the backbone of many of the smarter automation tools available today.

In essence, AI brings the power of learning, decision-making, and high accuracy to automation technologies. These capabilities enable AI-powered systems to mimic humans in performing tasks, revolutionizing the way businesses operate.

The AI revolution is not just changing the business workflows and contributing to digital transformation efforts - it’s changing the world.

### The Role of Robotic Process Automation (RPA)

results.content:
Never Stop Improving: AI integration is an ongoing process that requires continuous improvement and optimization. This requires keeping up with new developments and trends in AI, as well as regularly assessing and updating your workflows to ensure they are optimized for maximum efficiency and performance.

By following these steps, you can successfully integrate AI into your workflows and realize the many benefits it offers to your business. By integrating AI into your workflows, you can unlock the full potential of automation, data analysis, and decision-making. Start your journey today and discover how Decisions can help transform your organization with the power of AI orchestration.

### AI Agents Aren’t Broken. Your System Boundaries Are.

Process Orchestration

AI / Machine Learning [...] 1. Increased Efficiency: AI automates many time-consuming tasks, freeing up your team to focus on strategic, high-value work. By automating repetitive or low-value tasks, you'll boost efficiency, reduce errors, and save valuable time and money.  
     
   Example: By integrating AI into inventory management, a retail company can automate tracking, analysis, and restocking processes, eliminating manual inventory checks, reducing stock-outs, and ensuring well-stocked shelves with popular items.
2. Improved Employee Satisfaction: By automating repetitive tasks, you offer your employees the opportunity to focus on more engaging, fulfilling work. This leads to increased job satisfaction, improved work-life balance, and more space for innovation.

results.title: 7 AI-Powered Automations to Free up your Work Hours - dotSolved
results.content:
4. AI Customer Support Chatbots  
 Implement AI chatbots on your website or customer service channels with tools like Intercom, Drift, or Zendesk, which can handle routine inquiries, provide information, and escalate complex issues, reducing the need for constant manual intervention.

5. Content Creation and Optimization  
 Utilize AI writing assistants like Jasper or Grammarly to assist in drafting, editing, and optimizing content for emails, reports, blog posts, and social media, streamlining the content creation process.

6. Automated Data Entry and Processing  
 Employ AI based RPA tools such as UiPath for tasks like extracting data from documents, processing invoices, and entering data into systems, significantly reducing manual data entry work. [...] dotSolved

Contact us [...] In conclusion, AI-powered automations are revolutionizing the way we approach daily tasks in the workplace. By automating routine processes such as email management, meeting scheduling, task prioritization, customer support, data entry, content creation, and data analysis, organizations can achieve higher efficiency, reduce human error, and save valuable time. Implementing these AI tools not only enhances productivity but also allows employees to redirect their efforts toward more strategic and creative endeavors. As AI technology continues to advance, the potential for further optimization and innovation in our workflows is boundless.
